repo_prior_art.cite: none; CausalSmith doc/API.md Section 3 only records Manski bounded-outcome wrappers, not spell-history Lipschitz envelopes

published_prior_art.cite: McShane1934/Whitney1934 finite Lipschitz extension; KimKwonKwonLee2018 smooth treatment-response partial-ID envelopes

repo_prior_art.cite: closest analogues are pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 support-function sharpness and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v3 LP threshold, but neither has reversible spell-history metric or full observed-law cell-vertex compatibility

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 gives generic finite-discrete sharp/epsilon-sharp optimization; the proposed Farkas/Hall certificate is an analytic specialization

repo_prior_art.cite: closest analogues are pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 drift-radius phase diagram and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v3 threshold, but not reversible-spell finite-rho tightening

published_prior_art.cite: KimKwonKwonLee2018 and ManskiPepper2018 already establish smoothness/bounded-variation tightening; novelty is survival under serial response-type compatibility
Han2021 studies finite dynamic treatment identification; this is a conditional Cesaro limit once Conjecture 1 holds

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the flagship gap is still an analytic specialization of generic finite partial-ID/Farkas machinery; add a published open-problem anchor or a genuinely new full-class symbolic threshold beyond Duarte-style optimization and compare HonoréTamer2006 dynamic panel bounds.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The definition of R(P,rho,T) and sigma only preserves cell marginals and realized consistency; it does not impose the anchor moment constraints mu_t(h)=a_t(h) for observed histories, so Theta_I may include laws violating Assumption observed-anchor consistency.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The relative norm ||lambda||_{1,rel} is described but not defined as an explicit quotient norm/nullspace, leaving kappa_T(P;rho) not fully pinned as a scalar certificate.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: P is introduced as a finite-horizon law of (Y_t,D_t,X_t)_{t=1}^T, but the long-run Cesaro statement takes T to infinity without defining an infinite/projective law or a sequence P_T.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add anchor-moment constraints to the cell-vertex LP, or redefine mu_t(h) as the conditional mean among cells with H_t=h; then restate kappa_T using the same feasible set.
Define ||lambda||_{1,rel} explicitly as an infimum over the annihilator of the affine hull, and give the finite-dimensional LP used to compute kappa_T and rho-dagger.

To reach flagship, replace the one-target boundary diagnostic with a full reversible-spell symbolic threshold or cite a published open problem showing this dynamic Lipschitz sharpness regime was unresolved beyond generic optimization.
Restate Conjecture 3 over an infinite panel law or a projectively consistent sequence P_T, with eventual-periodic and stationary cases as separate corollaries.

recommended_next_step: Revise the feasible-set definition and kappa normalization first; then sharpen the published-axis gap enough to justify flagship rather than field tier.
